# pi-sidecar

> Add AI coding agents to any application via a lightweight REST service and async Python client.

## Getting Started

- [Quickstart](quickstart.md): Start the sidecar server and make your first AI call using the Python client in under a minute.

## User Guides

- [Managing AI Conversations](managing-conversations.md): Create stateful sessions, handle multi-turn prompts, and isolate context with agent directories.
- [Configuring Model Providers](configuring-providers.md): Discover and authenticate models from built-ins, ACPX (Cursor), and CLI providers.
- [Extending Capabilities with Tools](extending-with-tools.md): Equip the AI with built-in filesystem commands or integrate custom REST APIs via the HTTP tool executor.
- [Orchestrating Subagents](orchestrating-subagents.md): Delegate parallel or chained tasks to specialized agents using the built-in subagent extension.

## Recipes

- [Python Integration Patterns](python-integration-patterns.md): Drop-in code snippets for parallel execution, usage tracking, and robust async Python workflows.
- [Server Deployment Scenarios](server-deployment-scenarios.md): Configure watchdog health-checks, programmatic TypeScript startup, and secure local host bindings.

## Reference

- [REST API Endpoints](rest-api.md): Complete HTTP request and response contract for sidecar sessions and model discovery.
- [Python Client Reference](python-client.md): Public interfaces and typing for SidecarClient, AIResult, and async helper functions.
- [Environment Variables](environment-variables.md): Lookup table of all sidecar configuration options, extension path overrides, and log levels.

## Internals

- [Runtime Architecture](runtime-architecture.md): Understand the internal AgentSessionRuntime, ACPX module caching, and subagent process handling.
